7985	  \label{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}
7986	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_complex_iso}
7987	  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The iso is to the AUGMENTED concrete section
7988	  % complex D'_aug := (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT to the bare section complex.
7989	  % The evaluated augmented complex D carries Γ(V,F) at its augmentation node (def:cech_augmented_complex
7990	  % prepends F there), which the bare section complex does not have; the two agree only after the
7991	  % section complex is augmented along ε. The prover re-signs the Lean declaration to this augmented
7992	  % target.
7993	  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
7994	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmented_complex,
7995	    def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
7996	  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\). The augmented {\v C}ech complex of
7997	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} evaluated at \(V\) (through
7998	  \(\operatorname{toPresheaf}\)) --- call it \(D^\bullet\), the cochain complex of abelian
7999	  groups whose augmentation node is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) and whose {\v C}ech degree-\(p\)
8000	  term is \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) --- is isomorphic, as a
8001	  cochain complex, to the \emph{augmented} concrete section {\v C}ech complex
8002	  \[
8003	    D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}} \;:=\;
8004	    \bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F})\bigr).\operatorname{augment}
8005	      \varepsilon\, h_\varepsilon
8006	  \]
8007	  built from the concrete section {\v C}ech complex over the restricted family
8008	  \(U'_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V\) (degree-\(p\) term
8009	  \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), alternating coface restriction
8010	  differential) by prepending the augmentation node \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along
8011	  \begin{itemize}
8012	    \item the restriction product map \(\varepsilon : \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \to
8013	      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), \(t \mapsto \bigl(t|_{U_i \cap V}\bigr)_i\)
8014	      --- the evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation
8015	      \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}; and
8016	    \item the augmentation identity \(h_\varepsilon : \varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\), the
8017	      evaluation at \(V\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}.
8018	  \end{itemize}
8019	  Concretely: at the augmentation node both complexes carry \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\),
8020	  identified by the identity map; at {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism
8021	  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p) \cong \prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
8022	  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}; and these degreewise maps
8023	  intertwine the two differentials, including the augmentation differential \(\varepsilon\).
8024	\end{lemma}
8025	\begin{proof}
8026	  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
8027	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
8028	  The augmentation node of both complexes is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), matched by the
8029	  identity. At {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism between
8030	  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) and \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
8031	  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}. It remains to check the
8032	  degreewise isomorphisms commute with the differentials.
8033	
8034	  \emph{{\v C}ech differentials (degrees \(p \geq 0\)).} The differential of the section
8035	  complex is, read through the product equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\)
8036	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), the alternating sum of presheaf face
8037	  restrictions \(\sum_i (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) computed in
8038	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}. The differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the
8039	  evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech-nerve coface maps, assembled from the same push--pull
8040	  restriction comparisons; under the degreewise identification each coface of \(D^\bullet\)
8041	  becomes the corresponding face restriction, so the alternating sums agree. Reusing the
8042	  alternating-sum bookkeeping of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} --- rather than
8043	  rebuilding it --- matches these differentials.
8044	
8045	  \emph{Augmentation differential.} The lowest differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the evaluation
8046	  at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\)
8047	  (Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}); read through the degree-\(0\) instance of
8048	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} it is exactly the restriction product map
8049	  \(t \mapsto (t|_{U_i \cap V})_i\), which is the augmentation map \(\varepsilon\) of
8050	  \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) by construction. Both augmented complexes are well-formed by the
8051	  shared identity \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}).
8052	  Hence the degreewise maps assemble to an isomorphism of cochain complexes
8053	  \(D^\bullet \cong D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
8054	\end{proof}
8055	
8056	\begin{lemma}
8057	
8058	\leanok
8059	[The section {\v C}ech complex inside a cover member is contractible]
8060	  \label{lem:cechSection_contractible}
8061	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_contractible}
8062	  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The contractible complex is the AUGMENTED section
8063	  % complex D'_aug = (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT the bare section complex D'
8064	  % (which is not contractible: a one-member cover {V} gives H⁰(D') = Γ(V,F) ≠ 0). The prover
8065	  % re-signs the Lean declaration to Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0. The dep* engine supplies only the
8066	  % positive-degree homotopy; the augmentation node is a separate explicit degree-0 computation,
8067	  % given below.
8068	  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex,
8069	    def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
8070	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
8071	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the projection homotopy
8072	  % $h(s)_{i_0\ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0\ldots i_p}$ contracting the extended
8073	  % {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L83--112).
8074	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
8075	  % at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
8076	  % There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
8077	  % Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
8078	  % Let us define a homotopy
8079	  % $h : \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
8080	  % \longrightarrow \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}$
8081	  % by the rule $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
8082	  % In other words, $h : \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}, \mathfrak p} \to M_\mathfrak p$
8083	  % is projection onto the factor
8084	  % $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$ ..."
8085	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
8086	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof, the projection homotopy).}
8087	  Suppose \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) for some fixed index
8088	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), i.e.\ \(V\) lies inside one cover member. Then the \emph{augmented}
8089	  concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) of
8090	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} --- the section {\v C}ech complex over the family
8091	  \(U'_j = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,j \cap V\), augmented at
8092	  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) --- admits a
8093	  contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\).
8094	\end{lemma}
8095	\begin{proof}
8096	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex, def:cech_augmentation,
8097	    lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
8098	  Because \(V \le U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the restricted family \(U'_j = U_j \cap V\) has a
8099	  \emph{maximum}: \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\), and every
8100	  \(U'_j \le V\). Consequently, for each multi-index \(\sigma\),
8101	  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap U_\sigma \cap V = U_\sigma \cap
8102	  V = U'_\sigma\): prepending the index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) does not change the open, so the
8103	  prepend map is the \emph{identity} on each coefficient \(\Gamma(U'_\sigma, \mathcal{F})\)
8104	  (the membership criterion \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\) of
8105	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex}). The contracting homotopy is the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
8106	  map, \(h(s)_\sigma = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma}\), exactly the projection homotopy of the
8107	  Stacks proof; it splits into two parts.
8108	
8109	  \emph{Positive {\v C}ech degrees \((p \ge 1\)).} On the section {\v C}ech part the
8110	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine --- \(\operatorname{depDiff}\),
8111	  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\), \(\operatorname{depHomotopy\_spec}\),
8112	  \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (here cited only as the
8113	  Lean home of the \(\operatorname{dep\ast}\) engine declarations --- its standard-cover {\v
8114	  C}ech-vanishing \emph{conclusion} is \emph{not} a mathematical dependency of the present
8115	  argument) --- supplies the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy \(h\) with
8116	  \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\), since its unit, shift, and coface-commutation
8117	  compatibilities all hold with the prepend map acting as the identity on each coefficient.
8118	
8119	  \emph{Augmentation node (the piece the engine does not supply).} The engine governs only the
8120	  {\v C}ech degrees; the contracting-homotopy identity at the augmentation node must be checked
8121	  directly. The relevant homotopy component runs from the lowest {\v C}ech term
8122	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) down to the augmentation term
8123	  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), and is the \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-th coordinate projection
8124	  \(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} : (s_j)_j \mapsto s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\) --- well defined because
8125	  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = V\), so \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \Gamma(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V,
8126	  \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\). We verify
8127	  \(\varepsilon \circ \pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} + (\text{degree-}0\ \text{engine term}) = \mathrm{id}\)
8128	  on \(s \in \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\). The augmentation differential
8129	  \(\varepsilon\) is the restriction product, so
8130	  \(\bigl(\varepsilon(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} s)\bigr)_j = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\); the
8131	  degree-\(0\) contribution of the {\v C}ech homotopy supplies the complementary term
8132	  \(s_j - s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\) coming from the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) shift on
8133	  the {\v C}ech part. Their sum is \(s_j\), i.e.\ the identity. (This is the bottom node of the
8134	  Stacks projection homotopy: \(h\) on \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to M\) is projection onto the
8135	  factor \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}} = M\).) Together the two parts give a homotopy
8136	  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\) of cochain complexes.
8137	\end{proof}
8138	
8139	\begin{lemma}
8140	[Local vanishing of the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech homology]
8141	  \label{lem:cechSection_isZero_homology}
8142	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_isZero_homology}
8143	  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cechSection_contractible,
8144	    lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
8145	  Let \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) for some index \(i\). Then for every
8146	  degree \(p\) the homology \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) of the augmented {\v C}ech complex evaluated at
8147	  \(V\) (the complex \(D^\bullet\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}) is a zero
8148	  object; equivalently \(D^\bullet\) carries a contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}
8149	  \simeq 0\). This is the residual obligation of Step~3 of
8150	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}.
8151	\end{lemma}
8152	\begin{proof}
8153	  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cechSection_contractible,
8154	    lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
8155	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} the evaluated complex \(D^\bullet\) is
8156	  isomorphic to the augmented concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
8157	  The hypothesis \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) places \(V\) inside one cover
8158	  member, so Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_contractible} gives a contracting homotopy
8159	  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\); transporting it across the complex
8160	  isomorphism yields \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet} \simeq 0\).
8161	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero} then makes
8162	  \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) a zero object in every degree.
8163	\end{proof}
8164

8167	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward sections are sections over the preimage]
8168	  \label{lem:pushforward_obj_obj_mathlib}
8169	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj}
8170	  \mathlibok
8171	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
8172	  For a morphism \(f : X \to Y\), an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{M}\), and an open
8173	  \(U \subseteq Y\), the sections of the pushforward \(f_*\mathcal{M}\) over \(U\) coincide
8174	  definitionally with the sections of \(\mathcal{M}\) over the preimage:
8175	  \(\Gamma(U, f_*\mathcal{M}) = \Gamma(f^{-1}(U), \mathcal{M})\).
8176	\end{lemma}
